structured_fields.name: icd_codes
structured_fields.description: Exact ICD-10 and ICD-11 codes for this disease and its primary variants

structured_fields.name: subtypes
structured_fields.description: Known classification subtypes of this disease, each with name, brief description and severity level

example.name: Cutaneous Lupus
example.description: Affects primarily the skin with rashes and lesions

example.name: Systemic Lupus Erythematosus
example.description: Multi-organ involvement including kidneys, heart and brain

structured_fields.name: historical_milestones
structured_fields.description: Key historical events in the understanding and treatment of this disease — ordered chronologically

example.event: Term lupus erythematosus first used by Cazenave
LE cell phenomenon discovered by Hargraves

freetext_fields.name: definition
freetext_fields.description: A clear, clinically grounded definition of the disease — what it is, what systems it affects, and how it is broadly characterised

freetext_fields.name: nomenclature_notes
freetext_fields.description: Brief note on any naming conventions, historical name changes, or regional terminology differences worth flagging

prompt_hints.tone: clinical, authoritative, accessible to a medical but non-specialist audience
avoid: inventing ICD codes — only use verified codes
prefer: specific organ systems named in definition
severity levels that reflect clinical consensus
